name: Haldi Doodh (Golden Milk)
tagline: Turmeric milk with black pepper and ghee — curcumin's bedtime friend

hero.alt: Golden-yellow turmeric milk in a brass cup with a stick of cinnamon
hero.caption: haldi doodh — turmeric, pepper, ghee

steps.title: Warm the milk
steps.detail: Pour milk into a small pot. Heat on medium until steaming (don't boil hard — keeps the proteins gentle).
steps.tip: Plant milk works too — almond, oat, or soy.

steps.title: Whisk in spices and ghee
steps.detail: Add turmeric, black pepper, cinnamon, cardamom, and ghee. Whisk vigorously for 30 seconds — the ghee should melt and the spices fully combine.
steps.tip: Whisk well — undissolved turmeric leaves a chalky bottom layer.

steps.title: Sweeten and serve
steps.detail: Pour into a cup. Let it cool 1 minute, then stir in honey or jaggery. Drink warm, ideally 30 minutes before bed.
steps.tip: Honey added below 60°C — preserves enzymes and antioxidants.

healthFacts: Curcumin (from turmeric) reduces inflammation markers including hs-CRP.
Black pepper's piperine increases curcumin bioavailability by ~20×.
Curcumin is fat-soluble — fat (ghee or whole milk) is required for absorption.
Milk delivers calcium, B2, B12, and tryptophan — supports bone health and sleep.
